02 / Personal Projects

Built on
My Own Time

01
Novus Care
A sick day inspired this. When you're home alone and losing track of symptoms, the anxiety isn't just physical — it's the fear that you won't be able to explain any of it to a doctor. Novus Care turns fragmented plain-language logs into structured, bilingual clinical summaries, so nothing gets lost between the couch and the exam room.
AI Health App · Gemini 3 Hackathon 2025
02
2025 Concert Wrapped
Ticketmaster didn't ship a 2025 recap. So I built one. A Spotify Wrapped-style interactive mini-site that turns a year of live shows into a personal visual archive — artist stats, venue trails, the moments worth keeping. Built with Gemini and ChatGPT. Now planning the 2026 lineup.
Interactive Web · Personal · 2025

BI · Analytics · [ Year ]
Meredith Zhou · 2025 Analyst · Independent Product Developer
Senior Business Analyst
Cognesense · Hillside, IL · Mar 2025 — Present
  • Own enterprise Power BI analytics across ERP and CRM systems, establishing scalable semantic models and governed metrics that enable leadership decision-making across finance, sales, and operations.
  • Lead cross-functional initiatives — including post-acquisition process integration — by translating stakeholder input into structured business requirements and user stories, mapping lead-to-order workflows.
  • Drive periodic business forecasting and executive reporting cadences, producing monthly/quarterly KPI packages and board-ready views while reducing report preparation time by 70%.
  • Establish KPI and data governance standards; partner with IT and data owners across multiple ERPs to resolve source-of-truth gaps and streamline data quality.
  • Deliver operational analytics — backlog reviews, project time tracking, and value-chain mapping — contributing $1.3M in pull-forward impact across the parent company and its portfolio brands.
Graduate Marketing Consultant
QIAGEN · Germantown, MD · Jun — Aug 2024
  • Led strategic analysis for the QIAstat-Dx Franchise by evaluating product performance, customer adoption patterns, and operational bottlenecks. Synthesised insights into a 3-year growth strategy supporting portfolio prioritisation.
  • Conducted comprehensive market research in molecular diagnostics, assessing demand drivers, clinical use cases, and regional growth opportunities in a $200–400M market.
  • Partnered with cross-functional teams (Marketing, Finance, R&D) to build scenario models for market size, adoption curves, and operational capacity planning.
  • Designed and implemented Tableau dashboards to monitor key financial and operational KPIs — net margins, gross profit, inventory turnover — across QIAGEN's product lines.
  • Designed a data-driven approach to analyse FDA immunisation data for market segmentation, enhancing targeted strategies and go-to-market planning.
Brand Communication Intern
L'Oréal China – MG · Shanghai · Jan — Jul 2021
  • Authored scripts, coordinated site set-up, and managed weekly livestreaming sessions — drove over ¥1M CNY in sales and attracted over 1M viewership during promotional livestreaming.
  • Grew the streaming account from 3 to 6 channels during the internship through consistent performance analysis and iterative content strategy.
  • Compiled and analysed livestream data daily, reporting to the line manager and operations team to support decision-making and performance reviews.
  • Supported purchase order and internal order coordination using SAP ERP system, ensuring seamless business operations.
  • Collaborated with vendors to organise and host brand media events and PR packages for product line renewals.
2025 Concert Wrapped
Interactive Web · Personal Project · 2025 · ↗ View Live
  • Ticketmaster didn't ship a 2025 recap — so I built my own. A fully interactive Spotify Wrapped-style mini-site documenting a full year of live shows.
  • Features a coast-to-coast venue trail map, artist stats, concert receipt, and a "Netflix IRL" section — all styled with original design language.
  • Built end-to-end using Gemini 3 and ChatGPT through vibe coding — translating a clear product vision into functional, shareable interactive web.
  • Covered Seventeen (×3), Yiruma, Laufey, Wendy, Ronny Chieng, Hasan Minhaj, Guenther Steiner, and David Tao across TAC, CHI, BOS, DC, and NYC.
  • Now planning the 2026 lineup. Better seats incoming.
Novus Care
AI Health App · Gemini 3 Hackathon · 2025
  • Built entirely through vibe coding — no traditional programming background, just clear product logic and iterative natural language instructions to an LLM.
  • Core AI engine uses Google AI Studio to map natural language symptom descriptions ("my head feels like it's throbbing") into clinical-grade, bilingual medical summaries.
  • Generates physician-ready reports that break language barriers in the exam room — users can input in their mother tongue and output in the universal language of medicine.
  • Implemented strict safety logic gates to ensure the AI functions as a translator and organiser, never a diagnostic tool.
  • Built a bilingual state management system in React so outputs remain consistently professional regardless of input language.
2025 Concert Wrapped
Interactive Web · Personal Project · 2025  ·  View Live ↗
  • Ticketmaster didn't ship a 2025 recap — so I built one. A fully interactive mini-site that transforms a year of live shows into a personal visual archive.
  • Spotify Wrapped-style design: artist stats, venue trails, show count breakdowns, and the moments worth keeping — all in one scrollable experience.
  • Built entirely through vibe coding with Gemini 3 and ChatGPT, translating a clear product vision into functional code through iterative natural language.
  • Now planning the 2026 concert lineup — the site is evolving into an ongoing personal tracker.

5—9
After hours

This is what I do
when I'm not working

Clay on my hands, flour on the counter,
miles before sunrise, and a camera rolling.
The other half of who I am.

01 / Hobby

Pottery

[ Photo 1 ]
[ Photo 2 ]
[ Photo 3 ]
[ Photo 4 ]
View gallery →
Back to the
wheel
I first touched clay at six years old and spent years going to pottery classes — learning to centre, to pull, to let the form emerge. Then school took over, and the wheel went quiet for a long time.
In 2023 I came back to wheel throwing, and it felt like finding something I hadn't realised I'd missed. The muscle memory was still there, somewhere. It's become a hobby I genuinely look forward to every week.
02 / Hobby

Baking

[ Photo 1 ]
[ Photo 2 ]
[ Photo 3 ]
[ Photo 4 ]
View gallery →
Sundays smell
like butter
Baking is where precision meets instinct. I follow a recipe until I know it well enough to break it — a little more salt, a longer proof, a different flour. The kitchen is one of the few places where I'm happy to slow down and wait.
